post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 151: Team Fortress 2 Mafia


If you are dead or are not in this game, please do not post in this thread.

Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Story
"We've failed, men. Each and every one of you has failed."
How many times must I say that? Everybody knows how pathetic you all are. You have let us down far too many times, men. You are the sorriest excuses for soldiers I have ever seen! But this time, we will not fail. We are at Double Cross, and we will capture the enemy Intelligence. There have been too many losses for our team. We will not take that! The enemy is expecting us to be weaklings. Let us take advantage of that! However, we have a problem. Boys, we have two traitors! Two RED Spies have infiltrated our base, disguised as loyal followers. We will have to hunt them out. Move up! Move up! We will dominate these pathetic Spies!

-BLU Soldier





yes I know CTF is like the worst gamemode ever but idc it fits the flavor well


Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes small font size, white-colored font, and others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, do so before submitting your post into the thread.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.
  • Do not attempt to abuse or exploit any of the rules.
  • Note that there are no ICs in this game. SEs, please be on your best behavior.


Voting/Lynches
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames. Do not attempt to abuse this rule.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. In the case of a tie, the lynch is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.
  • If at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.


Activity/Day/Night
  • Each day phase will last 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
  • Each night phase will last for 72 hours.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day. Do not post until the moderator makes the post announcing the end of night, even if the deadline for night passes. To show you have read all the rules, make a post in your Role PM that states your role and flavorname.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • Each player must make at least 10 posts per day phase. Failure to do so will result in a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • If any player commits an excessive amount of rule infractions, they will be force-replaced. If the infraction committed is severe enough and impacts the game to an extent where force-replacing would not solve the problem, such as a player posting a screenshot of their Role PM in thread, I will be forced to modkill that player. Please do not make me modkill you as it affects the game adversely.


Moderators
  • I (ShineBunny) am the Moderator of Game 151. jeffisepic is the co-moderator of Game 151.
  • Red-colored text (#c00) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Orange-colored text (#ff9a00) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Blue-colored text (#3D85C6) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.


Setup
This game will use the Matrix6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons

Doctors cannot self-protect.
Note that if applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action. The power of the 1-Shot Bulletproof Townie cannot be roleblocked.
If the Mafia Roleblocker is the only remaining mafioso alive, he/she may perform both a kill and a roleblock during Night.


Role PMs
BLU Scout (Town Tracker) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],

You are BLU Scout (Town Tracker). While the BLU Heavy may laugh at you for being small, you really take it away when it comes to agility. With your extreme speed and ability to double jump, you can maneuver yourself to places where others would not be able to go. These attributes allow to you follow one player in the night to see who, if any person, they visited. Each night, you can track a player. You will see who that person targeted, if any. The thread can be found [link].

You win when all the RED Spies (Mafia) are killed.
BLU Heavy (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],

You are BLU Heavy (1-Shot Bulletproof Townie). As the big boy of the team, you naturally enjoy eating a lot. Your favorite food? Sandviches, of course! With your great bulk and survivability, you want to stay alive more, so with the help of your pal the trusty BLU Medic, you've managed to develop a Sandvich that you can eat to save yourself from dying should you be attacked by a Spy. Unfortunately, you only possess one of these, and so can only save yourself from death once. You will be immune to a single night kill. Your power is reflexive and cannot be roleblocked. The thread can be found [link].
You win when all the RED Spies (Mafia) are killed.
BLU Demoman (Town Jailkeeper) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],

You are BLU Demoman (Town Jailkeeper). You've discovered an excellent bar near Double Cross where nobody can resist going to for free. With a decent amount of money with you, each night you can treat somebody to several drinks there. They will become intoxicated enough that they will be unable to do whatever they might have done otherwise that night. Since that person will also have left their room, they will be safe from being killed from the RED Spies as well. Each night, you can jail one player who will be both roleblocked and protected.

You win when all the RED Spies (Mafia) are killed.
BLU Engineer (Town Cop) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],

You are BLU Engineer (Town Cop). You're excellent with machines, and as such, you've managed to develop a prototype of a new spy-checking device. Your complex new invention is a miniature camera which you can place in a room at night without its owner noticing. This way, you can see the true alignment of one player. You only have one camera, but you can use it as many nights as you want. Each night, you can investigate one player and will be notified of their alignment at the beginning of the next day. The thread can be found [link].

You win when all the RED Spies (Mafia) are killed.
BLU Medic (Town Doctor) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],

You are BLU Medic (Town Doctor). It doesn't matter at all that you have no actual medical knowledge, your motivation is enough. With your trusty Medi Gun, each night you can heal one player other than yourself to make them immune to any dirty tricks the RED Spies might be up to. Once a night, you may PM me a person's username. That person will then be protected from getting killed that night. The thread can be found [link].

You win when all the RED Spies (Mafia) are killed.
BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],

You are BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie). You are a very strong-minded person. With a burning passion to redeem the BLU team from being the object of ridicule of many others, you are here to root out the RED Spies from your base and take the enemy Intelligence to show the BLU team's worth. Being very vocal and confronting, your strength comes in your voice and your vote. The thread can be found [link].

You win when all the RED Spies (Mafia) are killed.
RED Spy (Mafia Goon) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],

You are a RED Spy (Mafia Goon). You've infiltrated the BLU base disguised as members of their team. Those moronic BLU team people never learn to spy-check, do they? Your aim is to kill off enough of them to be able to steal their Intelligence and bring it back to your base. Each night, you and your fellow Spy [username] (Mafia [Goon, Roleblocker]) share a factional kill. During the night, you may discuss in [this quicktopic]. The thread can be found [link]

You win when the RED Spies (Mafia) are the only living players remaining, or when nothing can prevent it from happening.
RED Spy with a Sapper (Mafia Roleblocker) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],

You are RED Spy with a Sapper (Mafia Roleblocker). You've infiltrated the BLU base disguised as a member of their team. Those moronic BLU team people never learn to spy-check, do they? Your aim is to kill off enough of them to be able to steal their Intelligence and bring it back to your base. Each night, you and your fellow Spy [username] (Mafia Goon) share a factional kill. In addition, those pathetic BLU team members each need a Dispenser in their rooms to supply them, which you can disable temporarily with your Sapper, forcing that BLU team member to devote all their attention to fixing it, preventing them from doing anything during the night. During the night, you may discuss in [this quicktopic]. The thread can be found [link].

You win when the RED Spies (Mafia) are the only living players remaining, or when nothing can prevent it from happening.


Alive (1)
  1. pandabear10 (SE)


Dead (8)
  1. SupaHotSuKa, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) had his body mercilessly shot and left to the maggots on Day 1.
  2. whatshisbucket, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) had his throat slit on Night 1.
  3. azduncan atomicpianowitch, RED Spy (Mafia Goon) had his head bonked in on Day 2.
  4. Fvr_Vain, BLU Scout (Town Tracker) was headshotted on Night 2.
  5. bigmath, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) was vote-kicked on Day 3.
  6. bennihana, BLU Medic (Town Doctor) was backstabbed on Night 3.
  7. Archimedes15, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) was bored to death of no flavor on Day 4.
  8. SFGiantsFan, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) was endgamed on Day 4.


Significant Events
SupaHotSuKa, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) was lynched Day 1.
whatshisbucket, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) was killed Night 1.
atomicpianowitch, RED Spy (Mafia Goon) was lynched Day 2.
Fvr_Vain, BLU Scout (Town Tracker) was killed Night 2.
bigmath, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) was lynched Day 3.
bennihana, BLU Medic (Town Doctor) was killed Night 3.
Archimedes15, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) was lynched Day 4.
SFGiantsFan, BLU Soldier (Vanilla Townie) was endgamed Day 4.


Warnings
Fvr_Vain (2), for posting in red three times and for impersonating the mod (#488, #492, #493)
bigmath (1), for posting twice during night (#502, #503)



Do not post until this line is crossed out.


You may now post. Day 1 begins now and ends on November 3 at 10:45 PM PDT.

post_rendered:
Mafiascum Wiki wrote:
However, a Day 1 claim strategy has been developed that purports to benefit town by limiting scum's options later on. The gist is that the BP (if any) claims, followed by the Tracker if there isn't one. If there's a BP, they become confirmed town for at least two days (assuming scum does not counterclaim, as 1v1s generally benefit town). If there isn't, the Tracker knows there's a Doctor and can play Follow the Cop (though being a Tracker vs two scum means this isn't perfect), or the Jailkeeper knows he has no interference; either way scum cannot get away with claiming BP later in the game.

If there isn't, the Tracker knows there's a Doctor and can play Follow the Cop (though being a Tracker vs two scum means this isn't perfect), or the Jailkeeper knows he has no interference; either way scum cannot get away with claiming BP later in the game.[/quote][/quote]

Funny how you left out the last sentence, which says, [quote=MafiaScum Wiki]While the jury is still out as to whether this strategy is truly helpful to town's winrate, most agree that the newbie setup should not have such a fixed claim strategy present, and so discussion is current on how to fix the problem. Backup6 is the leading candidate.[/quote]

The discussion on Backup6 is unimportant as we have Matrix6, but the point is that good gameplay is better here than just getting claims out.

post_rendered:
At any rate, even if claiming benefits town, this isn't a great practice for games in general. Before this is misinterpreted, let me explain. Suppose that claiming BP really does swing the game in favor to scum. Clearly this will be because of the game design. How much of it was from actual gameplay? Hint, it's not a positive number. (0) The point of newbie games is to learn to play actual Mafia. Suppose we used the setup from the good old days of 5 VT's, 1 Doc, 1 Cop, 2 Mafia. (This was the setup, right?) Suppose that we pull Follow the Cop and win. Cool, we won. How much actual Mafia gameplay did we learn? Practically none. If you just want to win (from the Game Theorist's perspective) and if this claim is actually true then we all claim. But is this good for actually learning, precisely what a newbie game is for? No. (Honestly, Backup6 is a more interesting setup and seems less broken, but that's beside the point.) So, no, I don't think claiming is a good idea, not only for lack of evidence but also because it's not in the spirit of Newbie games to do so. (I'd argue it's not in the spirit of any game, but that's beyond this argument.)

Fvr_Vain wrote:
if no one claims bp then town have more info on the setup, some PRs might even know the whole setup (JK and Tracker)

At the same time, Mafia know more on the setup. Also, even if JK or Tracker know full setup, what can they gain from that? No one else knows this information? Saying what the setup is reveals them to be PR's and the Mafia can take them down (unless it's self-protecting JK).

On the other hand, Mafia, if they know the setup, are not obliged by any means to share what they know. Since they are in the minority, knowing what Town PR's exist is huge for them. Scum may play differently with setup 1 than setup 3, for example. Point is, Mafia know what to look for. Town PR's, believe it or not, have their own tells. (jony/abean used this IIRC in 130 to narrow Vig to three out of like 10 people, correctly!)

Point is, Mafia gain more from knowing the setup than town.

JK knowing the setup allows him to know that he prevented the kill. (if there is no BP in this case).
Or he can try to protect the BP to make him live until the endgame, note that town have 85% winrate if he lives until the endgame. (if there is one)
Tracker knowing the setup allows him to play follow the "tracker". (if there is no BP)
Or he can be sure that all Night actions are scum. (if there is one)

post_rendered:
also this
the long version of why we shouldn't no lynch as explained by NeilOnnsu the EC (Experienced Challenged - Like it? :D)
NeilOnnsu wrote:
I've always wanted a place to post this, and I just realized that this might be good.

gerenjie PM'd me once asking me why it is that we always tell Newbies that we should lynch instead of no-lynch Day 1, along with some other excellent questions about Mafia theory that I never got around to answering (sorry, gerenjie). But after some reflection, I did figure out some pretty strong reasons for lynching Day 1 versus no-lynching, and I outlined it as follows:
NeilOnnsu in PM to gerenjie wrote:
Mislynching D1:

Start of D1: 7 to 2
Town kills
N1: 6 to 2
Scum kills
D2: 5 to 2
Town kills
N2: 4 to 2
Scum kills
D3: 3 to 2 LyLo

No-lynching D1:

Start of D1: 7 to 2
No-lynch
N1: 7 to 2
Scum kills
D2: 6 to 2
Town kills
N2: 5 to 2
Scum kills
D3: 4 to 2 MyLo
Nothing
N3: 4 to 2
Scum kills
D4: 3 to 2 LyLo

The end result is the same, and in fact, discussion time has been prolonged in the second case; however, the major difference is the people who end up surviving.

For the purposes of abstraction, we assume that the seven members of the Town can be ranked based on helpfulness/non-scumminess, as follows: A, B, C, D, E, F, G, with A being the most helpful and the least scummy. The first scenario results in a Town kill (G), a Scum kill (A), a Town kill (F), and a Scum kill (B), which means that for LyLo, Town will most likely be made up of C, D, and E. The second scenario results in a Scum kill (A), a Town kill (G), and two Scum kills (B, C), which means that for LyLo, Town will most likely be made up of D, E, and F. Clearly, the first situation is more advantageous.

Overly simplified, but you get the gist of the argument -- we want the Town kill:Scum kill ratio to be as high as possible if we want to end up successful (also, you can see from this argument that this is much more of an issue when there are very few PRs). Hope that helps!


I would recommend reading the above but the short version is if we don't lynch D1 it ends up that when town is in LyLO (the point of the game where town must lynch correctly or they lose) better players are left standing.

The other stealthily crept into another room. Chuckling evilly, he flipped open his butterfly knife. Quickly he drove it into the unfortunate whatshisbucket's throat. He laughed quietly at the death throes of the BLU Soldier caught unawares. "At least you died for honor -- and for my amusement! Maybe your colleagues will send a man next time." And he quickly went back into his room.

post_rendered:
Story
The remaining RED Spy was unperturbed by his accomplice's death. As night fell, he laughed to himself, remembering the previous day how the BLU team had decided to celebrate prematurely. He got up with his revolver in hand and walked into Fvr_Vain's room only to find him wide awake. Screaming, Fvr_Vain tried to run, but the RED Spy coolly aimed and shot him in the head, laughing, "Weren't you supposed to be good at dodging? Nothing personal, I just had to shut you up." Quickly he retreated to his room and assumed his disguise once more, joining the rest of the BLU team as they came running to the scene of the death. Mingled in with them, he chuckled inside himself at their amateurishness. Nobody suspected him for the death of the BLU Scout.

Fvr_Vain was headshotted Night 2. He was BLU Scout (Town Tracker).
